characters.silhouette: lean figure in unrelieved black, head slightly forward as if listening
broad, upright, deep-red robe; the only one who never hurries
pale gown, chin high, hands held very still
small, pale-gold dress, a book or bundle clasped at the chest
stooped grey-green, ear cocked, a man shaped by leaning toward keyholes
muted teal traveller's coat, weight even on both feet, watchful and quiet
near-translucent steel, edges that don't quite resolve, lit from within
one figure in matched livery; two names spoken by a single body, never turning its head between them

scenes.title: THE BATTLEMENTS
scenes.set: The bare platform reads as a castle rampart: the upstage wall is a low parapet, a single cold gap of open sky beyond it.
scenes.lighting: moonlight only, hard and blue, one shaft of pale light marking the threshold the Ghost will lead him across

beats.anchor: These indeed seem, for they are
beats.movement: Hamlet and Horatio stand close at center, two figures alone against the cold sky; neither moves.
beats.cue: lights up cold and low; faint wind under the dialogue

beats.movement: The Ghost enters at the upstage parapet, armed; Horatio turns up to it; Hamlet turns to face it.
beats.cue: wind drops to dead silence; the within-lit glow of the Ghost fades up at the parapet

beats.movement: Hamlet takes one step upstage toward the Ghost and holds; he does not close the gap.

beats.anchor: It beckons you to go away
beats.movement: The Ghost beckons and drifts a step toward stage left; Horatio moves in toward Hamlet to hold him.
Hamlet wrenches free, turning briefly back on Horatio who fails physically to keep him off the ledge.
Horatio withdraws into the dark stage right; Hamlet crosses the lit threshold toward the Ghost, the two now alone.
beats.cue: Horatio's exit; the pale threshold shaft brightens as Hamlet steps through it; everything else dims to near-black

beats.movement: Neither moves; Hamlet stands a single pace below and apart from the Ghost, studying whether it is him.
Hamlet sinks half a step lower toward the Ghost, held; the Ghost stays exactly where it is.
The Ghost drifts a half-step toward Hamlet for the account of the murder, the closest the two ever come.
beats.cue: the glow of the Ghost begins, very slowly, to pale

beats.anchor: O all you host of heaven!
beats.movement: The Ghost vanishes upstage; Hamlet is left alone at the threshold, facing the empty place it stood.
beats.cue: the Ghost's exit; its glow snuffs out; cold moonlight returns to the whole platform

beats.movement: Horatio returns from the dark stage right; Hamlet turns down and crosses toward him to center.
beats.cue: Horatio's re-entrance

beats.anchor: The time is out of joint.
beats.movement: Hamlet and Horatio join at center and exit together stage left.
beats.cue: exeunt; wind rises again as the platform goes dark

scenes.title: "TO BE OR NOT TO BE" / THE NUNNERY
scenes.set: The same platform, reframed as a castle lobby: the upstage parapet now reads as an interior gallery rail behind which the King and Polonius can hide.
scenes.lighting: warm interior wash, but a hard cold pool isolates Hamlet for the soliloquy; the hiding-place upstage stays half-lit so we always see the watchers

beats.movement: Claudius receives the single RG figure at center; Polonius and Ophelia wait apart, upstage right.
beats.cue: lights up warm; a low formal court chord

beats.movement: The one RG figure speaks both names in one breath without turning its head, then bows on 'To be commanded.'
RG withdraws stage left; Polonius crosses to place Ophelia downstage with her book, positioning her like a prop.
beats.cue: RG's exit

beats.movement: Claudius and Polonius retreat behind the upstage gallery rail to hide; Ophelia stays planted downstage.
beats.cue: the hiding-place light dims to a watching half-glow but never goes fully dark

beats.anchor: To be, or not to be:
beats.movement: Hamlet enters stage left into a cold isolating pool, not seeing Ophelia; he plays the soliloquy front, to no one.
beats.cue: Hamlet's entrance; warm wash drops out around him to leave a single hard cold pool

beats.movement: Hamlet sees Ophelia and crosses toward her along the downstage line; the cold pool widens to take them both.
beats.cue: the cold pool expands to include Ophelia

beats.movement: Ophelia steps in and holds out the bundle of remembrances to give them back; Hamlet stops just short of taking them.
Hamlet closes on Ophelia, hard and close; she holds her ground rather than recoil, the act of will kicked off its hinges.

beats.anchor: I have heard of your paintings
beats.movement: Hamlet breaks downstage and away from Ophelia, throwing the last of it back over his shoulder.
Hamlet exits stage left; Ophelia drifts to center alone and takes the inventory of the wreck, front, dry-eyed.
beats.cue: Hamlet's exit; the cold pool contracts to Ophelia alone, the watchers still dimly visible above her

beats.movement: Claudius and Polonius come forward from the gallery to center; Ophelia stands set aside, unconsulted.
beats.cue: warm watching light comes back up over the two men

beats.anchor: He shall with speed to England.
beats.movement: Ophelia exits stage right; Claudius and Polonius turn and exit stage left, the decision already made over her.
beats.cue: exeunt; blackout

scenes.title: THE MOUSETRAP
scenes.set: The same platform as a great hall; two facing thrones angled downstage-right for the King and Queen, a cleared playing square downstage-left where the dumb-show happens.
scenes.lighting: candle-gold court light, but the key light is rigged to favor Claudius's face; when the dumb-show plays, the audience's eyes are pulled to the King, not the players

beats.movement: Hamlet alone at center-left, setting his trap to the house; the playing square and empty thrones wait in low light around him.
beats.cue: lights up to a single warm pool on Hamlet; thrones and playing square held dim

beats.movement: Trumpets; the full court enters and settles — Claudius and Gertrude to the thrones upstage right, Ophelia downstage, Horatio holding the stage-left edge, RG at center, Polonius by the King.
beats.cue: trumpet flourish; full candle-gold court wash, brightest on the King

beats.movement: Hamlet crosses to Horatio at the stage-left edge and sets the watch on Claudius's face, aside.

beats.anchor: Lady, shall I lie in your lap?
beats.movement: Hamlet crosses to Ophelia and reclines at her feet downstage, deliberately placing himself with a sightline straight up to the King.
The dumb-show plays in the cleared square downstage-left while Hamlet narrates; no figure crosses the King's sightline.
beats.cue: a thin spill of light on the playing square, but the key light tightens on Claudius's face so the house watches him watch

beats.movement: Claudius stands sharply at his throne; every face on stage snaps up to him as the trap closes.
beats.cue: the dumb-show light cuts out; a hard isolating light slams onto Claudius alone

beats.movement: Claudius breaks from the throne toward the upstage exit; the court rises in alarm behind him on 'Lights, lights, lights!'
beats.cue: house light on the court flares chaotically up and out

beats.movement: All but Hamlet and Horatio rush out the upstage exit; Hamlet springs up and crosses to Horatio, the two alone and lit.
beats.cue: mass exit; lights collapse to a tight two-shot on Hamlet and Horatio at stage left

beats.movement: The single RG figure returns from the upstage exit and approaches; Hamlet turns to face it.
beats.cue: RG's re-entrance; the two-shot opens to take in the returning figure

beats.movement: Hamlet takes up a recorder and holds it out to RG as a real object before it becomes the metaphor.
Hamlet presses in on the RG figure with the pipe, naming it as a what and not a who.
RG withdraws upstage; Horatio slips off stage left; Hamlet turns alone to the house for the witching-time resolve, then exits stage left.
beats.cue: RG and Horatio exit; light narrows to a cold single pool on Hamlet, then blackout on his exit

scenes.title: THE CLOSET
scenes.set: The same platform as the Queen's private closet: the upstage parapet now hung with an arras (a heavy curtain) for Polonius to hide behind; two framed pictures implied downstage.
scenes.lighting: intimate low lamplight, warm and close; the Ghost's entrance brings back the cold within-lit blue from Scene One

beats.movement: Polonius coaches Gertrude, then steps behind the arras at the upstage wall to listen.
beats.cue: lights up warm and low; Polonius's silhouette just readable behind the arras

beats.movement: Hamlet enters stage left and crosses to confront his mother at center.
beats.cue: Hamlet's entrance

beats.anchor: Come, come, and sit you down;
beats.movement: Hamlet seizes Gertrude and forces her down to sit, blocking her path so she cannot leave.
Polonius cries out behind the arras; Hamlet wheels upstage and thrusts his sword through the curtain.
beats.cue: a sharp stab of cold light on the arras at the sword-thrust

beats.movement: Polonius falls dead behind the arras; Hamlet turns from the curtain back to Gertrude, the body unseen for now.
Hamlet lifts the arras and discovers Polonius dead, then turns back down to his mother.
beats.cue: the arras pulls aside; Polonius's body revealed in a cold slant of light

beats.movement: Hamlet drags Gertrude downstage to the two implied portraits and holds her face to them, comparing the brothers.
beats.cue: lamplight tightens to a close warm pool on mother and son at the downstage edge

beats.movement: Hamlet rounds on the picture of Claudius, the indictment rising, Gertrude breaking under it.

beats.anchor: Save me, and hover o'er me
beats.movement: The Ghost enters at the upstage wall; Hamlet turns and rises toward it, Gertrude seeing nothing.
beats.cue: the Ghost's entrance; the cold within-lit blue of Scene One bleeds back over the warm lamplight

beats.anchor: How is it with you, lady?
beats.movement: The Ghost holds upstage, pitying Gertrude; Hamlet turns between the Ghost above and his mother below.
The Ghost steals away toward the upstage portal; Hamlet follows it with his arm, Gertrude staring at vacancy.
The Ghost exits at the portal; Hamlet turns back down to Gertrude, the cold blue draining away.
beats.cue: the Ghost's exit; the cold blue withdraws and the warm lamplight returns alone

beats.movement: Hamlet comes down to Gertrude, gentling, and takes the scene to its quiet 'Good night, mother.'
Hamlet drags out Polonius's body toward the stage-left exit; he and Gertrude part severally.
beats.cue: exeunt severally; slow fade to black

scenes.title: THE GRAVEYARD; THE DUEL; THE END
scenes.set: The same platform as a churchyard, then cleared for the duel: a low mound of earth downstage-left for the grave, a small table upstage-right for the wine and foils.
scenes.lighting: flat grey overcast for the graveyard, warming to gold candle-light for the court duel, then bleeding to a final cold blue as the bodies fall

beats.movement: Hamlet kneels at the grave-mound downstage-left and takes up the skull; Horatio stands close.
beats.cue: lights up flat and grey; no warmth anywhere

beats.movement: Hamlet sets down the skull and rises, turning the thought toward Horatio.
Hamlet and Horatio withdraw into the dark stage left; Claudius enters alone, lit, the one place his surface drops with no one to manage.
beats.cue: Hamlet and Horatio exit; a single warm pool isolates Claudius against the grey

beats.anchor: Thou wouldst not think how ill
beats.movement: Claudius moves upstage to set the foils at the table; Hamlet and Horatio re-enter downstage for the quiet 'readiness is all.'
beats.cue: Claudius crosses to prepare the foils; Hamlet and Horatio re-enter

beats.anchor: Give them the foils, young Osric.
beats.movement: Claudius and Gertrude assemble at the table upstage-right; foils are brought and Hamlet takes one to the center.
beats.cue: the court warms up to full candle-gold for the duel; the wine cup set glinting on the table

beats.movement: Hamlet and the unseen opponent play; Hamlet scores the first hit at center stage.
Claudius takes up the cup at the table and drops the poisoned pearl, then offers it toward Hamlet.

beats.anchor: The queen carouses to thy fortune,
beats.movement: Gertrude lifts the poisoned cup and drinks the toast, then crosses toward Hamlet to wipe his face — fatal toast and tenderness kept as two separate gestures.
Claudius half-rises toward Gertrude and stops; he loses the fight against his own decency by a hair and lets it be too late.
Hamlet and the opponent are wounded with the envenom'd foil; Gertrude falls downstage-center and dies, the love arriving one second too late.
beats.cue: a first wash of cold blue begins to bleed into the gold as Gertrude falls

beats.movement: Hamlet rounds on Claudius, names the poison himself, and drives the envenom'd foil into him.
beats.cue: the cold blue takes more of the stage

beats.movement: Claudius dies and is lost in the dark; Hamlet sinks toward Horatio, who rushes in to catch him.
beats.cue: Claudius's death; the gold gone, only cold blue left over the three

beats.movement: Horatio reaches for the cup to follow his friend; Hamlet overrules him in one breath and commissions him to live and tell the story.
Hamlet dies in Horatio's arms; Horatio lays him down and stands alone at center, the only one left to hold the record.
beats.cue: Hamlet's death; light contracts to a single cold pool on Horatio and the body, then a slow fade as a dead march rises
